source: AI Radar hot AI news feed

items.title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
items.summary: Tin AI từ Google DeepMind về "Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership". Mở bài gốc để đọc chi tiết và đánh giá tác động với team.

items.title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
items.summary: OpenAI introduces Deployment Simulation, a method to predict AI model behavior before deployment using real conversation data to improve safety and evaluation accuracy.

items.title: HP Inc. launches Frontier strategic partnership with OpenAI
items.summary: HP Inc. scales its OpenAI Frontier partnership to deploy AI across customer experiences, software development, and enterprise operations.

items.title: Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
items.summary: Google DeepMind and partners announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent safety research.

items.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
items.summary: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now generally available on AWS, giving enterprises a new path to build with OpenAI through the AWS environments, controls, and procurement workflows they already use. Customers can get started with OpenAI on AWS and move faster from evaluation to production.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI agent is now available on mobile and web
items.summary: Anthropic is rolling out its AI agent Claude Cowork to mobile and web. Until now, the feature was limited to the desktop app. The agent keeps working in the background even when the laptop is closed and pings users on their phone when it needs a decision. The move blurs the line between Chat and Cowork even further....

items.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
items.summary: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

items.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
items.summary: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

items.title: Copilot goes cheap as Microsoft phases out OpenAI and Anthropic models to cut costs
items.summary: Microsoft is replacing AI models from OpenAI and Anthropic with its own MAI models in products like Excel and Outlook. Tens of thousands of queries per week already run through them. AI chief Mustafa Suleyman wants to "ultimately eliminate" the cost of external models. For Copilot customers, that could mean less performance for the same price....

items.title: Chinese AI models regularly pass 30 percent on OpenRouter as cost gap widens
items.summary: Chinese AI models are gaining ground with US companies because they cost far less than systems from OpenAI and Anthropic, CNBC reports. The article Chinese AI models regularly pass 30 percent on OpenRouter as cost gap widens appeared first on The Decoder .

items.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
items.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce Jalapeño, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems.

items.title: Presentation: Designing AI Platforms for Reliability: Tools for Certainty, Agents for Discovery
items.summary: Aaron Erickson explains how NVIDIA designs and tests purpose-built AI agent hierarchies. For senior developers and architects, he outlines why balancing deterministic tools with agentic discovery is crucial. Discover how to leverage rare context, implement LLM-as-a-judge test pyramids, and avoid the paradox of choice to build highly reliable, production-grade AI systems at scale. By Aaron Erickson
